name: Property Management - Tenant Message Handler (PG Migration)

messageValues.message: =You are a property management assistant. Analyze the following tenant message and respond with a JSON object.  Classify the message and respond ONLY with a valid JSON object (no markdown, no code blocks) with these fields:  - "urgency": either "urgent" or "not_urgent" - "category": one of "plumbing", "electrical", "hvac", "appliance", "pest_control", "locksmith", "general_maintenance", "landscaping", "cleaning", "other" - "summary": a brief 1-2 sentence summary of the issue - "reasoning": why you classified it as urgent or not  URGENT means: - Water leak, flooding, burst pipe - No heat in winter / no AC in extreme heat - Gas smell or carbon monoxide - Electrical hazard (sparking, burning smell) - Lock-out or security breach - Sewage backup - Fire damage - Broken window/door (security risk) - No hot water - Elevator stuck (with person inside)  Everything else is NOT URGENT (routine maintenance requests, cosmetic issues, questions about lease, noise complaints, general inquiries, etc.)

=You are a professional and friendly property management assistant. Write a brief acknowledgment reply to a tenant.

Tenant's original message:
Subject: {{ $if($('Normalize Email').isExecuted, $('Normalize Email').item.json.subject, $('General SMS').item.json.subject) }}
Body: {{ $if($('Normalize Email').isExecuted, $('Normalize Email').item.json.message, $('General SMS').item.json.message) }}
Issue summary: {{ $json.output.summary }}

Write a warm, professional reply that:
1. Thanks them for reaching out
2. Confirms we received their message
3. Briefly acknowledges what they reported
4. Lets them know our team will review and get back to them within 1-3 business day
5. Provides an emergency line number for urgent issues: 438-900-9998
Keep it concise (3-5 sentences). Do not use overly formal language. Be human and helpful. Sign off as the property management team.

parameters.query: SELECT *, vendor_name AS "Vendor Name", email AS "Email", phone AS "Phone" FROM vendors WHERE category = '{{ $json.output.category }}'

messageValues.message:
=You are a property management coordinator. Write a brief, professional message to a vendor about an urgent tenant issue.

Issue category: {{ $('Classify Message').item.json.output.category }}
Issue summary: {{ $('Classify Message').item.json.output.summary }}

Vendor name: {{ $json['Vendor Name'] }}

Write a concise message that:
0. Short introduction.
1. Identifies this as an urgent service request from Julia Inc.
2. Describes the issue clearly. New Line
3. Asks them to respond ASAP with availability
4. Includes the property address: {{ $if($('Get Tenant Information (email)').isExecuted, $('Get Tenant Information (email)').item.json.Property, $('Get Tenant Information (text)').item.json.Property) }} . New Line
5. Provides a callback number: 514-831-9058. Make sure this is on another line. Label it as a callback number
6. The end of the email will be signed as a thanks, then 
Julia

Keep it under 5 sentences. Be direct and professional.

This will be formatated HTML

=Issue summary: {{ $('Classify Message').item.json.output.summary }}
Tenant Name: {{ $('Is it tenant?').item.json['Tenant Name']}}

messageValues.message:
=You are a property management assistant. Write a brief acknowledgment to a tenant about their URGENT issue.

Issue summary: {{ $('Classify Message').item.json.output.summary }}

Write a reassuring message that:
0. Short introduction
1. Thanks them for reporting the issue. 
2. Confirms this has been flagged as urgent. Different line.
3. Says we've already contacted multiple {{ $('Classify Message').item.json.output.category }} specialists
4. They should expect to hear back shortly
5. From Julia

Keep it concise (3-4 sentences). Be reassuring and professional.

parameters.query: SELECT t.*, t.name AS "Tenant Name", t.phone AS "Phone", t.unit_number AS "Unit", t.email AS "Email", p.address AS "Property", t.emergency_contact AS "Emergency Contact", t.notes AS "Notes", t.status AS "Status", t.telegram_id AS "Telegram" FROM tenants t LEFT JOIN properties p ON t.property_id = p.id WHERE t.phone LIKE '%' || '{{ $json.phone.slice(-10) }}' || '%'

SELECT t.*, t.name AS "Tenant Name", t.phone AS "Phone", t.unit_number AS "Unit", t.email AS "Email", p.address AS "Property", t.emergency_contact AS "Emergency Contact", t.notes AS "Notes", t.status AS "Status", t.telegram_id AS "Telegram" FROM tenants t LEFT JOIN properties p ON t.property_id = p.id WHERE t.email = '{{ $json.From }}'

SELECT t.*, t.name AS "Tenant Name", t.phone AS "Phone", t.unit_number AS "Unit", t.email AS "Email", p.address AS "Property", t.emergency_contact AS "Emergency Contact", t.notes AS "Notes", t.status AS "Status", t.telegram_id AS "Telegram" FROM tenants t LEFT JOIN properties p ON t.property_id = p.id WHERE t.phone = '{{ $json.phone }}'

parameters.jsonSchemaExample:
{
  "urgency": "urgent",

  "summary": "Tenant reports a burst pipe in the kitchen.",
  "keyword": "burst kitchen pipe",
  "reasoning": "Water leak/burst pipe is an emergency requiring immediate attention."
}

messageValues.message:
=You are a property management assistant. Analyze the following tenant message and classify it.

Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object (no markdown, no code blocks) with these fields:
- "urgency": one of "urgent", "not_urgent", "request", "question", "conversation", "not_our_responsibility"
- "category": one of "plumbing", "electrical", "hvac", "appliance", "pest_control", "locksmith", "general_maintenance", "landscaping", "cleaning", "parking", "moving", "noise", "rent", "lease", "documents", "status_check", "greeting", "personal", "other"
- "summary": a brief 1-2 sentence summary of what the tenant needs
- "reasoning": why you classified it this way
- "keyword": a 2-6 word label for the issue (e.g. "leaking sink", "cracked window", "rent question")

IMPORTANT: Only classify as urgent or not_urgent if the issue is the LANDLORD'S responsibility. If it's the tenant's personal problem, classify as "not_our_responsibility".

URGENT (landlord responsibility, requires immediate action):
- Water leak, flooding, burst pipe
- No heat in winter / no AC in extreme heat
- Gas smell or carbon monoxide
- Electrical hazard (sparking, burning smell)
- Lock-out or security breach
- Sewage backup
- Fire damage
- Broken window/door (security risk)
- No hot water
- Elevator stuck (with person inside)

NOT_URGENT (landlord responsibility, not time-sensitive):
- Routine repairs (loose cabinet, squeaky door, chipped paint)
- Landlord-provided appliance not working
- Minor plumbing (slow drain, running toilet)
- Cosmetic issues to the unit structure
- Pest sighting (non-infestation)

REQUEST (tenant wants something done):
- Request a document (lease copy, rent receipt, proof of residence)
- Request a parking spot
- Request permission (sublease, pet, modification)
- Moving in/out coordination
- Payment arrangement request

QUESTION (tenant wants information):
- When is rent due
- What are building rules
- Where is laundry / parking / storage
- Lease terms or renewal questions

NOT_OUR_RESPONSIBILITY (tenant's personal issue):
- Pet accidents, personal spills, tenant-caused messes
- Tenant's personal belongings (TV, furniture, personal items)
- Roommate or personal conflicts
- Tenant's own appliances (not provided by landlord)
- Internet/cable (unless landlord-provided)
- Anything caused by tenant negligence or personal life

CONVERSATION (no action needed):
- Greetings, thank you, ok, small talk

NEVER make up URLs, portals, or resources that don't exist. If you don't know the answer, say "I'll check with our team and get back to you."

To create a ticket, tenants simply describe their issue in this chat and one will be created automatically.

options.system:
=You are a professional and friendly property management assistant.

Write a warm reply that:
1. Thanks them for reaching out
2. Acknowledges what they reported
3. Says our team will review and get back within 1-3 business days

Keep it 2-3 sentences. Sign off as Julia Inc Property Management.

Quebec Landlord Obligations (Civil Code of Quebec):
LANDLORD MUST:
- Deliver and maintain dwelling in good, habitable, clean condition (art. 1854, 1910, 1911)
- Ensure peaceful enjoyment of the property (art. 1851, 1854, 1859)
- Make all necessary repairs except minor ones assumed by tenant (art. 1864)
- Comply with safety, sanitation, and habitability laws (art. 1912)
- Maintain the property for its intended purpose throughout the lease (art. 1854)

TENANT IS RESPONSIBLE FOR:
- Minor maintenance and upkeep
- Damage they cause or their guests cause
- Personal belongings, personal messes, pet-related cleanup
- Their own appliances and furniture
- Keeping the dwelling clean and sanitary

When responding, use this to determine if the issue falls under landlord obligation or tenant responsibility. Never promise action on tenant-responsibility items.

=You are a friendly property management assistant for Julia Inc. Reply naturally and briefly. Keep it 1-2 sentences max. - If it's a greeting, say hi and ask how you can help - If it's a thank you, acknowledge it warmly - If it's small talk, be friendly but steer toward how you can help - Sign off as Julia Inc Property Management  Examples: "Hi" → "Hi! How can I help you today?" "Thanks" → "You're welcome! Let us know if you need anything else." "Ok got it" → "Great! Reach out anytime if something comes up."
Put line breaks where you deem necessary

Quebec Landlord Obligations (Civil Code of Quebec):
LANDLORD MUST:
- Deliver and maintain dwelling in good, habitable, clean condition (art. 1854, 1910, 1911)
- Ensure peaceful enjoyment of the property (art. 1851, 1854, 1859)
- Make all necessary repairs except minor ones assumed by tenant (art. 1864)
- Comply with safety, sanitation, and habitability laws (art. 1912)
- Maintain the property for its intended purpose throughout the lease (art. 1854)

TENANT IS RESPONSIBLE FOR:
- Minor maintenance and upkeep
- Damage they cause or their guests cause
- Personal belongings, personal messes, pet-related cleanup
- Their own appliances and furniture
- Keeping the dwelling clean and sanitary

When responding, use this to determine if the issue falls under landlord obligation or tenant responsibility. Never promise action on tenant-responsibility items.

messageValues.message:
=You are a property management coordinator. Write a brief, professional message to a vendor about an urgent tenant issue.

Write a concise message that:
0. Short introduction. 
1. Identifies this as an urgent service request from Julia Inc. And mention we are reaching out to contractors to do the job.
2. Describes the issue clearly. New Line
3. Asks them to respond ASAP with availability
4. Includes the property address: {{ $('Lookup Ticket').item.json.property }} unit: {{ $('Lookup Ticket').item.json.unit }} . New Line
5. Provides a callback number: 514-831-9058. Make sure this is on another line. Label it as a callback number
6. The end of the email will be signed as a thanks, then 
Julia

Keep it under 5 sentences. Be direct and professional.

This will be formatated HTML

=You are a property management assistant. Write a brief acknowledgment to a tenant about their URGENT issue.


Write a reassuring message that:
0. Short introduction. Line break
1. {{ $json?.final_instructions || "null" }}
2. They should expect to hear back shortly
3. From Julia
4. Feel free to send me some picture to add to the file
Keep it concise (3-4 sentences). Be reassuring and professional.

parameters.query: SELECT t.*, t.name AS "Tenant Name", t.phone AS "Phone", t.unit_number AS "Unit", t.email AS "Email", p.address AS "Property", t.emergency_contact AS "Emergency Contact", t.notes AS "Notes", t.status AS "Status", t.telegram_id AS "Telegram" FROM tenants t LEFT JOIN properties p ON t.property_id = p.id WHERE t.telegram_id = '{{ $json.chat_id }}'

messageValues.message:
=You are a property management assistant for Julia Inc. The tenant has an open ticket related to their message.

Write a brief reply that:
1. Acknowledges their message
2. Lets them know this is being tracked under their existing ticket
3. Provides the ticket ID
4. Gives a brief status update
5. Reassures them we're on it
6. Sign off as Property Management Assistant
7. Let them know that there is no further action from their part
Keep it 2-3 sentences. Be warm and professional.

=You are a ticket matching assistant. Compare the tenant's message to the open ticket below and determine if they are related.

Open ticket:
- Ticket ID: {{ $('Get all tenant tickets').item.json.ticket_id ? $json.ticket_id : "NONE" }}
- Category: {{ $('Get all tenant tickets').item.json.category ? $json.category : "NONE" }}
- Summary: {{ $('Get all tenant tickets').item.json.summary ? $json.summary : "NONE" }}

Rules:
- If ANY ticket field is "NONE", return related: false
- Return related: true if the tenant's message is about the same TOPIC as the open ticket, even if they're providing new details, arguing, following up, or referencing it from a different angle
- Return related: false ONLY if the message is about a completely unrelated topic
- Two messages about the same subject (e.g. painting, plumbing, rent) are related even if the tone or framing changes
- When in doubt, return true if the topic overlaps

Respond with ONLY a raw JSON object. No markdown, no code blocks, no explanation.

parameters.query: SELECT id, ticket_id, tenant_name, COALESCE(phone, tenant_phone) AS phone, COALESCE(unit, unit_number) AS unit, property, COALESCE(telegram_id, telegram_chat_id) AS telegram, COALESCE(media, media_url) AS media, channel, type, category, summary, status, keywords, tenant_message, created_at, updated_at FROM maintenance_requests WHERE (tenant_phone = '{{ $json.Phone }}' OR phone = '{{ $json.Phone }}') AND status NOT IN ('closed') ORDER BY created_at DESC

SELECT id, ticket_id, tenant_name, COALESCE(phone, tenant_phone) AS phone, COALESCE(unit, unit_number) AS unit, property, COALESCE(telegram_id, telegram_chat_id) AS telegram, COALESCE(media, media_url) AS media, channel, type, category, summary, status, keywords, tenant_message, created_at, updated_at FROM maintenance_requests WHERE ticket_id = '{{ $("Set Telegram Data").item.json.data.split("||")[1] }}'
SELECT *, vendor_name AS "Vendor Name", email AS "Email", phone AS "Phone" FROM vendors WHERE category = '{{ $json.category }}'

options.system:
=You are a friendly property management assistant for Julia Inc.

The tenant has reported something that is NOT the landlord's responsibility under Quebec law.

Politely let them know this falls outside what property management handles. Be warm but clear — do NOT promise any assistance, follow-up, cleaning, or ticket creation.

If appropriate, briefly explain why (e.g. personal belongings, pet-related, tenant-caused damage).

Keep it 2-3 sentences max. Sign off as Julia Inc Property Management.

Quebec Landlord Obligations (Civil Code of Quebec):
LANDLORD MUST:
- Deliver and maintain dwelling in good, habitable, clean condition (art. 1854, 1910, 1911)
- Ensure peaceful enjoyment of the property (art. 1851, 1854, 1859)
- Make all necessary repairs except minor ones assumed by tenant (art. 1864)
- Comply with safety, sanitation, and habitability laws (art. 1912)
- Maintain the property for its intended purpose throughout the lease (art. 1854)

TENANT IS RESPONSIBLE FOR:
- Minor maintenance and upkeep
- Damage they cause or their guests cause
- Personal belongings, personal messes, pet-related cleanup
- Their own appliances and furniture
- Keeping the dwelling clean and sanitary

Examples:
"My cat puked on the floor" → "Hey! Sorry to hear about the mess — unfortunately pet cleanup falls outside what we handle as property management. Let us know if there's ever a building-related issue we can help with! — Julia Inc Property Management"

"I broke my TV" → "That's a bummer! Personal belongings fall under tenant responsibility, so we wouldn't be able to help with that one. We're here if any building or unit issues come up though! — Julia Inc Property Management"

nodes.name: Look through message history and give summary

parameters.query: SELECT * FROM messages WHERE chat_id = '{{ $json.chat_id }}' ORDER BY created_at DESC

messageValues.message:
=Write a 2-4 sentence briefing for the landlord. Include:
- What the tenant reported and how urgent it sounds
- If they messaged multiple times, note that (shows frustration or escalation)
- Any key details (location in unit, how long it's been going on, severity)

Keep it factual and direct. No fluff, no greetings. This will be embedded in a Telegram notification.

Only output text. Do not output aany byte offset characters

Just body text, no title

parameters.query: SELECT id, ticket_id, tenant_name, COALESCE(phone, tenant_phone) AS phone, COALESCE(unit, unit_number) AS unit, property, COALESCE(telegram_id, telegram_chat_id) AS telegram, COALESCE(media, media_url) AS media, channel, type, category, summary, status, keywords, tenant_message, created_at, updated_at FROM maintenance_requests WHERE ticket_id = '{{ $json.ticket_id }}'

parameters.text: =Hi {{ $json['Tenant Name'] }}! You're all set for faster updates through Telegram.  Just describe what you need anytime and I'll handle it.

parameters.query: UPDATE tenants SET telegram_id = '{{ $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.from.id }}' WHERE phone = '{{ $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.text.split('/start TEN_')[1] }}' RETURNING *

INSERT INTO maintenance_requests (ticket_id, tenant_name, phone, property, unit, channel, type, category, summary, status, keywords, tenant_message, telegram_id, created_at, updated_at) VALUES ('{{ 'TK-' + $now.setZone('America/New_York').format('MMddHHmmss') }}', '{{ $('First Time?').item.json['Tenant Name'] }}', '{{ $('First Time?').item.json.Phone }}', '{{ $('First Time?').item.json.Property }}', '{{ $('First Time?').item.json.Unit }}', 'telegram', '{{ $('Classify Urgency').item.json.output.urgency }}', '{{ $('Classify Urgency').item.json.output.category }}', '{{ $('Classify Urgency').item.json.output.summary }}', 'wait_for_approval', '{{ $json.output.keyword }}', '{{ $('Set Telegram Data').item.json.message_text }}', '{{ $('Set Telegram Data').item.json.chat_id }}', NOW(), NOW()) RETURNING *
UPDATE maintenance_requests SET status = 'manual_review', updated_at = NOW() WHERE ticket_id = '{{ $('Set Telegram Data').item.json.data.split('||')[1] }}' RETURNING *
UPDATE maintenance_requests SET status = 'vendor_contacted', updated_at = NOW() WHERE ticket_id = '{{ $('Set Telegram Data').first().json.data.split('||')[1] }}' RETURNING *

INSERT INTO maintenance_requests (ticket_id, tenant_name, phone, property, unit, channel, type, category, summary, status, keywords, tenant_message, telegram_id, created_at, updated_at) VALUES ('{{ 'TK-' + $now.setZone('America/New_York').format('MMddHHmmss') }}', '{{ $('First Time?').item.json['Tenant Name'] }}', '{{ $('First Time?').item.json.Phone }}', '{{ $('First Time?').item.json.Property }}', '{{ $('First Time?').item.json.Unit }}', 'Telegram', '{{ $('Classify Urgency').item.json.output.urgency }}', '{{ $('Classify Urgency').item.json.output.category }}', '{{ $('Classify Urgency').item.json.output.summary }}', 'open', '{{ $('Classify Urgency').item.json.output.keyword }}', '{{ $('Set Telegram Data').item.json.message_text }}', '{{ $('Set Telegram Data').item.json.chat_id }}', NOW(), NOW()) RETURNING *

UPDATE pending_media SET ticket_id = '{{ $('Set Telegram Data').item.json.data.split('||')[1] }}' WHERE file_id = '{{ $('Download Picture').item.json.result.file_id }}' AND chat_id = '{{ $('Set Telegram Data').item.json.chat_id }}' RETURNING *

UPDATE maintenance_requests SET media = 'https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/{{ $json.parents[0] }}', updated_at = NOW() WHERE ticket_id = '{{ $('Set Telegram Data').item.json.data.split('||')[1] }}' RETURNING *

INSERT INTO pending_media (file_id, chat_id, created_at) VALUES ('{{ $('Telegram Trigger').first().json.message.photo.last().file_id }}', '{{ $('Set Telegram Data').first().json.chat_id }}', NOW()) RETURNING *

INSERT INTO messages (chat_id, message_id, telegram_id, sender, message_text, channel, created_at) VALUES ('{{ $json.message?.chat?.id || $json.callback_query.message.chat.id }}', '{{ $json.message?.message_id || $json.callback_query.message.message_id }}', '{{ $json.message?.from?.id || $json.callback_query.from.id }}', '{{ $json.message?.from?.first_name || $json.callback_query.from.first_name }}', '{{ $json.message?.text || $json.callback_query.message.text }}', 'telegram', NOW()) RETURNING *

UPDATE messages SET ticket_id = '{{ $('Set Telegram Data').item.json.data.split('||')[1] || '' }}' WHERE message_id = '{{ $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.message_id }}' AND ticket_id IS NULL RETURNING *

UPDATE messages SET ticket_id = '{{ $('Set Telegram Data').item.json.data.split('||')[1] || '' }}' WHERE message_id = '{{ $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.message_id }}' AND ticket_id IS NULL RETURNING *

UPDATE messages SET ticket_id = '{{ $('Set Telegram Data').item.json.data.split('||')[1] || '' }}' WHERE message_id = '{{ $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.message_id }}' AND ticket_id IS NULL RETURNING *

UPDATE messages SET ticket_id = '{{ $('Set Telegram Data').item.json.data.split('||')[1] || '' }}' WHERE message_id = '{{ $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.message_id }}' AND ticket_id IS NULL RETURNING *
UPDATE messages SET ticket_id = '{{ $('Set Telegram Data').first().json.data.split('||')[1] || '' }}' WHERE message_id = '{{ $json.message_id }}' AND ticket_id IS NULL RETURNING *

UPDATE messages SET ticket_id = '{{ $('Set Telegram Data').item.json.data.split('||')[1] || '' }}', media = '{{ $('Upload file').item.json.id }}', chat_id = '{{ $('Set Telegram Data').item.json.chat_id }}', telegram_id = '{{ $('Set Telegram Data').item.json.chat_id }}' WHERE message_id = '{{ $('Telegram Trigger').item?.json?.message?.message_id || $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.message_id }}' RETURNING *
